regions.us-ports: Today, an overcast sky with a 67% rain probability and west winds at 11 knots are expected to impact port operations and shore excursions in Charleston, Savannah, and Charleston, South Carolina, with rain expected to affect embarkation and disembarkation procedures. In the next 24 to 48 hours, northeast winds at 10 knots and a 40% chance of drizzle may affect Jacksonville, Miami, and Port Everglades, possibly impacting outdoor activities onshore. Beyond 48 hours, north winds at 11 knots combined with an 80% probability of rain showers are expected to affect Norfolk, Baltimore, and Boston, leading to likely disruptions in port operations and increased caution for ship maneuvers. All vessels should prepare for sustained winds near 10 to 11 knots and reduced visibility in precipitation at these affected ports. Passengers and crew should plan accordingly for wet conditions and potential delays in the US Ports region over the coming days.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, winds in Nassau are steady from the east-southeast at 5 knots under clear skies with zero percent rain chance, providing excellent conditions for embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions across the Bahamas including Freeport, Bimini, and the Berry Islands. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect increasing winds from the south-southeast to south at 9 to 11 knots with overcast skies and rain chances remaining at 1 percent in Nassau and surrounding ports such as Key West, Grand Cayman, and Ocho Rios, with no operational impacts anticipated. Beyond 48 hours, winds will strengthen to 16 knots from the south-southeast with continued overcast conditions and a 2 percent rain chance, affecting ports including Falmouth, Puerto Plata, La Romana, Santo Domingo, and Samaná, but still without disruption to cruise operations or excursions. All ports in the Central Caribbean and Bahamas should prepare for slightly breezier conditions while maintaining smooth port activities.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today an elevated rain chance of 72% with drizzle is expected to impact port operations and shore excursions primarily in San Juan and St. Thomas, where easterly winds at 10 knots and overcast skies will reduce visibility and create damp conditions. Current conditions in San Juan show light southwest winds at 2 knots with only a 21% rain chance, but the shift to easterly flow and increased rain probability will require extra caution during embarkation and outdoor activities. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, ports including St. Croix and St. Maarten will see easterly winds increasing to 14 knots with drizzle and a reduced rain chance near 25%, which may affect shore excursions but with less intensity than today. By 48 hours out, St. Kitts and Antigua will experience east-southeast winds sustained at 14 knots with drizzle and a 10% rain chance, indicating improving conditions with limited impact on cruise operations. Passengers and crew should remain prepared for wet conditions and variable visibility through May 1, with gradual easing of rain and steady trade winds supporting safer port operations beyond that timeframe.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, conditions in Cozumel feature clear skies with an east-southeast wind at 5 knots and a 0% chance of rain, allowing normal port operations and shore excursions without weather disruptions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect southeast winds increasing to 12 knots with overcast skies and a 37% rain probability that may affect Costa Maya and Belize City, potentially causing delays or modifications to some shore activities. By day 3, a 59% chance of thunderstorms with south-southeast winds at 14 knots is forecast for Roatan and Belize City, which may affect safe embarkation and disembarkation procedures and could disrupt shore excursions. Passengers and crew should monitor updates as winds strengthen and rain chances rise beyond 48 hours, especially in Belize City where increasing thunderstorm activity is expected to impact cruise operations.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today, elevated east winds of 15 knots with overcast skies are present in Aruba, supporting steady port operations and shore excursions with minimal disruption and 0% rain chance. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will increase to 18 knots east in Aruba and similarly affect Curacao and Bonaire, where sustained east winds near 18 to 20 knots are expected, with overcast conditions and drizzle probabilities below 12%, posing no significant rain impact. By Day 3, Aruba will see east winds rise to 20 knots with drizzle at an 8% chance, triggering a Small Craft Advisory for all Southern Caribbean ports including Cartagena, where elevated east winds near 20 knots may influence docking and tendering procedures. Passengers and crew should prepare for sustained moderate winds and reduced visibility from drizzle beyond 48 hours, but rain chances remain low enough not to affect shore excursions or port operations significantly.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today in the Lesser Antilles, east winds at 6 knots and overcast skies with a 1% rain chance will provide smooth conditions for embarkation and shore excursions at Barbados, St. Lucia, Martinique, Dominica, Antigua, St. Kitts, St. Maarten, St. Vincent, and Grenada. Port operations remain normal with minimal weather disruptions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, sustained east winds will increase to 10 knots with overcast skies and a 4% rain chance today, shifting to drizzle with a 6% rain chance on day two, primarily impacting Barbados, St. Lucia, and Martinique with light moisture but no expected operational delays. Beyond 48 hours, winds will strengthen to 13 knots from the east, accompanied by drizzle and a 31% rain chance that may affect shore excursions and tendering in Barbados and nearby ports such as St. Vincent and Grenada. All vessels should monitor conditions closely for potential minor impacts during this period.
regions.los-angeles: Today winds at Los Angeles and San Pedro are light from the west-southwest at 3 knots with clear skies and zero percent chance of rain, providing optimal conditions for embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions at all local ports including Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds increasing to 10 knots from the west-southwest with overcast skies and a 1 percent rain chance, which will slightly reduce sunshine but have no impact on port operations at Los Angeles, San Pedro, or Long Beach. By the second day, south winds at 9 knots will bring foggy conditions with a 1 percent rain probability, potentially lowering visibility for vessels approaching Catalina Island and Marina del Rey but remaining manageable for cruise ship operations. Beyond 48 hours, winds shift to the south-southwest at 9 knots with overcast skies and a 2 percent chance of rain, signaling continued reduced sky clarity without operational disruptions for all cruise ports in the region. Passengers and crew should anticipate steady moderate winds and mostly cloudy conditions while cruise schedules and shore excursions remain fully on track.
regions.ensenada: Today in the Ensenada region, winds are light from the east-southeast at 2 knots with clear skies and a 1% chance of rain, resulting in smooth conditions for embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions at Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect a shift to westerly winds around 11 to 12 knots and persistent overcast skies with rain probabilities remaining low at 2%, which will maintain steady but somewhat cloudier conditions primarily impacting Ensenada and Punta Banda ports. Beyond 48 hours, winds will veer slightly to the west-southwest near 9 knots with continued overcast skies and a 2% rain chance, suggesting stable marine conditions for all three ports with no operational disruptions anticipated. Passengers and crew should plan for consistent moderate westerly breezes while maintaining normal port and excursion schedules throughout this period.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today in the Cabo San Lucas region, winds are light from the north at 3 knots with overcast skies and a 0% chance of rain, resulting in smooth conditions for embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions at Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect west-northwest winds increasing to 10-12 knots with continued overcast skies and zero rain probability, which will maintain steady marine conditions for all three ports without weather-related operational impacts. Beyond 48 hours, winds will remain from the west-northwest near 10 knots, skies will stay overcast, and rain chances remain at 0%, supporting uninterrupted port activity and stable sea conditions for cruise vessels operating in Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz. No marine wind advisories or rain-related disruptions are forecast throughout this period. Passengers and crew can plan shore excursions and port operations confidently under these consistent conditions.
regions.mazatlan: Today in the Mazatlan region, winds are light from the east-northeast at 2 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent rain chance, providing smooth conditions for embarkation and shore excursions at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect a shift to southerly winds at 7 knots with overcast skies on Day 1, followed by westerly winds increasing to 8 knots and continuing overcast conditions on Day 2, which may slightly reduce visibility but will not impact port operations at any of the three ports. Beyond 48 hours, winds will veer northwest at 9 knots with persistent overcast skies, maintaining calm seas and stable conditions for all cruise activities across Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata. No rain is forecast during this period, so no weather-related delays or disruptions are anticipated. Crew and passengers can plan for consistent marine conditions during embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions throughout the next three days.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today in Puerto Vallarta, winds are light from the northeast at 2 knots with overcast skies and zero percent chance of rain, providing smooth conditions for embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions at Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will shift to the west-southwest at 10 knots with continued overcast skies and no rain expected, maintaining stable conditions for all listed ports without operational disruptions. Beyond 48 hours, winds increase slightly to 11 knots from the southwest while skies remain overcast and rain chances stay at zero percent, so cruise operations and shore activities should continue uninterrupted throughout the region. Passengers and crew can expect consistent visibility and calm seas during this period with no advisories in effect. We will monitor for any changes but currently see no weather threats impacting port or vessel operations through the next three days.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today, an overcast sky with southeast winds at 2 knots and a 43% rain chance may affect shore excursions and embarkation procedures in Barcelona, Valencia, Palma de Mallorca, and Ibiza due to elevated rain probability and reduced visibility. In the next 24 to 48 hours, east-northeast winds increasing to 16 knots with rain showers and a 23% rain chance could affect Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar, with intermittent showers likely disrupting some outdoor activities but not port operations significantly. By day three, east winds at 7 knots and a 20% rain chance indicate continued rain showers near Barcelona and Valencia, though impacts will be minimal. Crew should monitor wind shifts and overcast conditions closely as they may influence maneuvering in port, while passengers should plan for possible light rain during shore excursions across most Western Mediterranean ports.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today an 85% rain chance is expected to impact port operations and shore excursions in Naples, with northeast winds increasing to 12 knots and persistent rain reducing visibility during embarkation and disembarkation. Rain is also expected to affect Marseille, Nice, Monaco, Genoa, La Spezia, Livorno, Civitavecchia, Sardinia, Corsica, Split, Dubrovnik, and Venice where similar conditions will challenge outdoor activities. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, a northeast wind of 13 knots will maintain overcast skies with no rain expected, improving conditions notably in Naples and surrounding ports to include Civitavecchia and Genoa. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift to north at 11 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero rain probability forecasted, allowing smoother cruise operations and shore excursions throughout the Central Mediterranean. Passengers and crew should prepare for continued wet conditions today but anticipate clearer, drier weather moving into May 2nd.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today, skies over Athens (Piraeus), Santorini, Mykonos, Rhodes, Corfu, Istanbul, Izmir, Cyprus, Haifa, and Alexandria remain clear to overcast with light south to south-southwest winds around 1 to 7 knots, and no rain is expected, so port operations and shore excursions are proceeding without weather-related interruptions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, a 43% chance of rain with northeast winds near 9 knots may affect Athens (Piraeus), Santorini, and Mykonos, potentially impacting embarkation and shore excursions in these ports. Rhodes and Corfu should monitor conditions as northeast winds shift, though rain probability remains below 30%, posing no operational concerns there. Beyond 48 hours, winds are forecast to increase to 12 knots from the north-northeast with partly cloudy skies and only a 3% chance of rain in Athens, suggesting improved conditions for cruise operations and port activities across the Eastern Mediterranean. Passengers and crew should remain alert for updates regarding the moderate rain chance in select ports on day two.
